compiled_state.title: As a developer, the ticket CLI works correctly on Linux, macOS bash 3.2+, and Alpine/BusyBox CI
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Verify the library operates correctly across target platforms (Linux bash ≥4, macOS bash ≥3.2 default install, Alpine/BusyBox CI containers) and verify that tickets containing shell-special characters (quotes, backslashes, newlines, unicode) round-trip correctly through every write and read op. Specify the flock fallback strategy explicitly: `flock(1)` is a hard requirement on Linux and macOS (SC1/SC2 targets enforced); Alpine/BusyBox uses a documented `python3 fcntl.flock` fallback path with a relaxed SC1/SC2 slack budget (since the fallback reintroduces one `python3` startup). Document `realpath`/`sha1sum`/`md5sum` coreutils behavior differences where they matter.

**Why**: SC8 requires cross-platform correctness and the `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY` rollback as a safety net. Alpine/BusyBox is a real CI target for downstream consumers, and BusyBox historically lacks `flock(1)` by default. Punting fallback strategy to implementation time is risky — deciding it at design time means the cost is visible and the trade-offs are explicit.

**Scope**:
- IN: CI matrix across Linux (bash ≥4), macOS (bash ≥3.2, the Apple default), and Alpine/BusyBox; shell-special-char round-trip tests (quotes, backslashes, newlines, unicode) for every covered op; documented flock fallback strategy — Linux/macOS hard requirement on `flock(1)`, Alpine uses `python3 fcntl.flock` fallback with a slack budget that Story G honors; documented `realpath`/`sha1sum`/`md5sum` coreutils fallbacks for BusyBox where flags or behavior differ; `_ticketlib_has_flock` detection helper at library source-time.
- OUT: Performance regression suite (Story G); rewriting the library architecture; new subcommands.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the library loads and operates correctly on Linux (bash ≥4), macOS (bash ≥3.2), and Alpine/BusyBox CI containers — verified by a CI matrix job
  ← Satisfies: "The library operates correctly across platforms — Linux (bash ≥4), macOS (bash ≥3.2, default install), and Alpine/BusyBox CI containers"
- When this story is complete, every covered write and read op produces byte-accurate round-trips for tickets containing quotes, backslashes, newlines, and unicode characters — verified by randomized-content round-trip tests
  ← Satisfies: "Tickets containing shell-special characters (quotes, backslashes, newlines, unicode) round-trip correctly through all write and read operations"
- When this story is complete, the flock fallback strategy is implemented as specified: hard `flock(1)` requirement on Linux+macOS (SC1/SC2 enforced), `python3 fcntl.flock` fallback on Alpine/BusyBox with a Story-G-documented slack budget — verified by a test that simulates `flock`-absent conditions and asserts correct fallback activation
  ← Satisfies: "The library operates correctly across platforms ... with documented fallbacks when coreutils features differ"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Platform matrix must run on every CI build so platform drift surfaces quickly
- [Reliability] Alpine slack budget explicitly acknowledges the `python3 fcntl.flock` fallback reintroduces one Python startup — a trade-off for platform coverage

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
